name: Positions Book
summary: Retrieves the user's Positions Book via a POST call. On success it returns a JSON array of position objects (one per contract) covering day buy/sell, carry-forward, open quantities, net position, LTP, realized PnL and unrealized MTOM; on failure it returns a single JSON object.

request_params.name: jData*
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list.

json_fields.name: actid
json_fields.description: Account Id of logged in user.

response_fields.name: stat
response_fields.description: Position book success or failure indication.

response_fields.name: daysellavgprc
response_fields.description: Day buy average price. (per doc; appears to be Day Sell average price)

response_fields.name: cfsellavgprc
response_fields.description: Carry Forward Buy average price. (per doc; appears to be Carry Forward Sell average price)

response_fields.name: urmtom
response_fields.description: UnrealizedMTOM. (Can be recalculated in LTP update: = netqty * (lp from web socket - netavgprc) * prcftr)

response_fields.name: openbuyqty
response_fields.description: (no description given in doc) Open Buy Quantity.

response_fields.name: opensellqty
response_fields.description: (no description given in doc) Open Sell Quantity.

response_fields.name: openbuyamt
response_fields.description: (no description given in doc) Open Buy Amount.

response_fields.name: opensellamt
response_fields.description: (no description given in doc) Open Sell Amount.

response_fields.name: openbuyavgprc
response_fields.description: (no description given in doc) Open Buy average price.

response_fields.name: opensellavgprc
response_fields.description: (no description given in doc) Open Sell average price.

response_fields.name: pp
response_fields.description: (no description given in doc) Price precision.

response_fields.name: request_time
response_fields.description: This will be present only in a failure response. (In the dedicated failure-response table on page 29 its description is given as: Response received time.)

response_fields.name: emsg
response_fields.description: Error message. (Present only in a failure response.)

notes: Success response is a JSON ARRAY of objects (one per contract); failure response is a single JSON object. The base URL is https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI; the curl example uses a 'BaseURL' placeholder. Query params (jData, jKey) are sent as a url-encoded form body separated by '&', with jData being the JSON object. urmtom can be recalculated on LTP updates as: netqty * (lp from web socket - netavgprc) * prcftr. prcftr = gn*pn/(gd*pd). request_time and emsg appear only in a failure response. The source actually has TWO separate failure tables: (1) the success response-fields table lists request_time with the description 'This will be present only in a failure response.'; (2) a dedicated failure-response table (page 29) lists stat (Not_Ok, 'Position book request failure indication.'), request_time ('Response received time.') and emsg ('Error message.'). DOC INCONSISTENCY: daysellavgprc is described as "Day buy average price" and cfsellavgprc as "Carry Forward Buy average price" in the source table — these descriptions appear to be copy-paste errors (should read Day Sell / Carry Forward Sell average price respectively). The sample success response also contains a 'prarr' field (value 'C') which is not documented in the response-fields table, while several documented fields (token, totsellavgprc, bep, instname) do not appear in the sample. The fields openbuyqty, opensellqty, openbuyamt, opensellamt, openbuyavgprc, opensellavgprc, mult, and pp have BLANK description cells in the source table (page 28); their meanings here are inferred from their names.
issues: openbuyqty, opensellqty, openbuyamt, opensellamt, openbuyavgprc, opensellavgprc, mult, and pp have blank Description cells in the source table (page 28) — their meanings above are inferred from their names. The doc has self-contradictory descriptions for daysellavgprc ("Day buy average price") and cfsellavgprc ("Carry Forward Buy average price"), noted in notes. The 'prarr' field in the sample success response is undocumented. Field 'token' is listed as a response field but does not appear in the sample. request_time and emsg appear only in failure responses. All curl/JSON blocks transcribed verbatim from the cleaned text and visually confirmed against the page images. CORRECTION vs draft: the draft had appended '(Response received time.)' directly into request_time's success-table description; that parenthetical actually belongs to the separate failure-response table on page 29 — clarified the two-table provenance rather than conflating them.
